title: QUIC Connection Runtime Deadline Scheduling Verification
verification_method: Focused unit tests and inspection evidence from the shard-owned deadline scheduler and runtime timer helpers.
expected_result: The scheduler dequeues earlier due ticks first, breaks equal due ticks deterministically, ignores stale generations after a rearm, and the runtime exposes generation-aware arm and cancel effects while treating stale expirations as a no-op.
procedure: Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover timer ordering, deterministic tie-breaking, stale-generation suppression, and runtime timer expiry handling.
Confirm the scheduler's stale-registration checks reject older generations after a rearm or cancellation.
Confirm timer effects continue to use monotonic due ticks and the runtime accepts only matching-generation expirations.
scope: This slice covers deadline scheduling and the timer-effect plumbing around the runtime. It does not prove the RFC 9000 idle-timeout value derivation or any future close/drain policy math beyond the scheduling seam.
preconditions: The CRT requirement traits are discoverable by the test runner.
The deadline scheduler remains shard-owned and the runtime timer helpers remain internal to the repository.
